v1alpha1.description: KusoAddon is a managed dependency (Postgres, Redis, ...) belonging to a KusoProject.

secretKeys.description:
Optional allowlist of keys to mirror. Empty =
mirror every key from the source secret.

secretName.description:
Name of an existing kube Secret in the project
namespace. Its keys are mirrored into the addon's
<name>-conn secret.

passwordSecret.description:
Reference an existing Secret containing the addon
password. The chart copies the value at install
time into <release>-conn instead of generating
a random one. Leave unset to let the chart
generate a random 24-char password.

pooler.description: Opt-in PgBouncer connection pooler in front of a kind=postgres addon. Reach it via the POOLER_HOST/POOLER_PORT/POOLER_URL keys in the addon's <name>-conn Secret; DATABASE_URL stays direct. Ignored for non-postgres kinds.

authMode.description: How PgBouncer authenticates clients. "" / "userlist" (default) = static userlist from the addon's single conn-secret role (dedicated single-addon pooler). "query" = auth_query against the backend's pg_shadow via a privileged auth user — for the shared cluster-DB pooler fronting many per-project roles.

instancePooler.description: Marks this as the shared pooler for the cluster (instance) Postgres, so the chart renders it even though the CR sets no useInstanceAddon.

publicTCP.description: Opt-in public TCP endpoint. When enabled and the cluster's Traefik install has a configured TCP entrypoint pool, kuso allocates one port from the pool, renders a Traefik IngressRouteTCP, and the addon becomes reachable on the cluster's public IP at that port. Admin-only — a public database is a real attack surface. The allocated port lives in spec.publicTCP.port (server-stamped).

port.description: Server-allocated TCP port from the configured pool. 0 = unallocated. Set by the kuso server; do not edit by hand.

singleNode.description: Only honoured when ha=true on a kind=postgres addon. Relaxes the CNPG pod anti-affinity so the 3 replicas may colocate on one kube node — accepts the SPOF in exchange for HA-shaped replication on a single-node cluster (dev / staging). Default false: replicas land on different nodes.

tls.description: In-cluster wire TLS for a native kind=postgres addon. "disable" (default) = plaintext, conn advertises sslmode=disable — works with every driver, zero config. "require" = the StatefulSet serves TLS via a self-signed cert and the conn advertises sslmode=require, for apps that mandate encrypted DB connections in production (Go/pgx, Rails, strict prod-mode checks). Default node-postgres rejects a self-signed cert under require, so only opt in when your driver accepts an unverified chain or you pin the CA.

useInstanceAddon.description:
Name of an instance-shared addon (registered by
admin via instance secret keyed
INSTANCE_ADDON_<UPPER>_DSN_ADMIN). The server
creates a per-project database on the shared
server and writes its DSN into <name>-conn. No
StatefulSet is rendered.

webUI.description:
Opt-in: expose the addon's built-in web console (mailpit's mail viewer, NATS monitor, …) through the kuso server as an authenticated reverse proxy at /api/projects/

/addons//webui/. No new ingress, no per-UI password — access is gated by the caller's kuso session. Kinds without a known UI port silently no-op.
